title: ‘State-sanctioned cruelty’: Chelsea family sues ICE after Mother’s Day arrest last year
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A Chelsea family filed a lawsuit against ICE after a violent arrest incident on Mother's Day in 2025.
The family alleges ICE agents used excessive force, traumatizing the children during 'Operation Patriot' in Massachusetts.
ICE did not respond to the lawsuit, which claims repeated patterns of unaccountable behavior by federal agents.

title: In rare sightings, scientists spot blue whales in waters off Martha’s Vineyard
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: New England Aquarium scientists reported rare sightings of blue whales in southern New England waters near Martha's Vineyard.
Two blue whales were observed just 15 miles south of Martha's Vineyard, marking their first documentation in this area by the aquarium.
The sightings suggest favorable ocean conditions for blue whales seeking food, potentially indicating a shift in their distribution.

title: One person found, one still missing after fishing boat overturns off Cape Cod
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The Yankee Rose fishing boat capsized off Cape Cod, leaving one person unconscious and another missing.
The incident occurred three nautical miles northeast of Race Point, Provincetown, Thursday, per the U.S. Coast Guard.
A recent previous emergency on the Yankee Rose involved carbon monoxide poisoning, leading to two hospitalizations.

title: Fenway location of Regina Pizzeria has closed
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Regina Pizzeria's Fenway location at 1330 Boylston St. has closed after the lease was not renewed.
The Fenway location opened in 2013 as part of Regina Pizzeria's expansion throughout Boston and Massachusetts.
Regina Pizzeria now operates 12 locations, many of which are quick-service rather than sit-down restaurants.

title: Mansfield man killed in crash on I-95 in Attleboro, officials say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Chad Askew, 43, of Mansfield died after his Honda Civic hit a tractor-trailer on I-95 North in Attleboro.
The crash occurred near Exit 4 on I-95 North at approximately 5:20 a.m., according to the Attleboro Fire Department.
Authorities are searching for a man seen fleeing the scene wearing shorts and boots; investigation by Massachusetts State Police is ongoing.

title: Newly released video shows crash that left pickup truck dangling over Zakim Bridge
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts DOT released video of a crash where a pickup dangled on Zakim Bridge.
Crash occurred on Feb. 5 when driver swerved to avoid a disabled vehicle, state police report.
No injuries were reported as a snowbank and fence prevented the truck from falling off.

title: A Mass. college is buying a local golf course. Here’s why.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Stonehill College is purchasing the 150-acre Easton Country Club to support athletics and academics.
The golf course will remain a semi-private venue, accessible to Stonehill and the local community.
Stonehill plans to use the course for academic opportunities and to expand its NCAA Division I golf programs.

title: A deported dad, a city in chaos, and a Tinder sting
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Christina Toledo and her deported husband Nexan Aroldo Asencio Corado face family relocation decisions after his detention by ICE.
Reporter Chris Muther recounts witnessing chaos in Puerto Vallarta after a drug cartel leader's death ignited violent reactions.
Patricia Wen's article delves into a failed Tinder sting operation by college students, leading to unintended real-life consequences.

title: Boston restaurant hit with brief license suspension after hosting Patriots players’ raucous party
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Estella restaurant in Boston had its liquor license suspended for hosting a party with Patriots players and strippers.
The Boston Licensing Board suspended the license for one day due to violations including indoor smoking and unauthorized entertainment.
Owner Helder Brandao blamed Patriots players for bringing hookahs and liquor, though he accepted responsibility for the incident.

title: Netflix acquires Ben Affleck’s AI startup InterPositive
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix acquires InterPositive, an AI tools startup co-founded by Ben Affleck to aid filmmakers.
InterPositive's AI tools are designed to protect and expand creative choices in the filmmaking process.
Ben Affleck views AI as a craftsman, capable of performing laborious filmmaking tasks to lower production costs.
